title: DataAuditor Configuration Schema
description: JSON Schema for validating DataAuditor audit configuration files (v1.0.0)

sources.description: Data sources for the audit (left=reference source, right=target source)

right.description: Target/comparison data (typically the data to validate)

unpivot.description: Optional configuration for unpivoting (wide to long format transformation)

join.description: Join configuration for matching records between left and right sources

keys.description: List of key field pairs to join on. Records are matched when all key pairs are equal.

filters.description: Optional filters to apply to sources before processing. Each filter removes records that do not match the filter criteria.

source.description: Source to apply the filter to

value.description: Single value to compare against (used with operator)

values.description: List of values for multi-value filter (record passes if field is in this list)

rules.description: Audit rules to validate data consistency between sources. Rules are evaluated only on matched record pairs (not orphans).

logic.description: Logical operator for combining conditions. AND: rule fails if ANY condition fails. OR: rule fails if ALL conditions pass.

rule_type.description: Type of rule. OK: rule validates coherence (passes when conditions are met). KO: rule validates incoherence (fails when conditions are met).

conditions.description: List of conditions to evaluate. Each condition compares two sides (fields or values).

operator.description: Comparison operator. equals: A=B, differs: A≠B, greater: A>B, less: A<B, contains: B∈A, not_contains: B∉A

target_value.description: Fixed value to compare against (alternative to target_field). If both specified, target_value takes precedence.

tolerance.description: Tolerance for numeric comparisons. Condition passes if |A - B| <= tolerance.

normalize.description: Normalization function to apply before comparison

show_matching.description: Whether to include matching records (OK results) in report output

max_diff_preview.description: Maximum length of field value preview in report (in characters). Longer values are truncated.

source.description: Data source configuration. Defines how to read and parse data from a file.

format.description: File format. csv/txt/dat: delimited text. fixed_width: positional fields. xlsx: Excel. json/jsonl: JSON (full file or line-delimited).

label.description: Human-readable label for the source (used in UI and reports)

color.description: UI color identifier for visual distinction (e.g., 'rose', 'blue', 'green')

encoding.description: Character encoding of the source file (utf-8-sig handles Excel BOM)

delimiter.description: Field delimiter for delimited formats (csv, txt, dat). Examples: ';', ',', '	'

has_header.description: Whether the file has a header row with field names

skip_rows.description: Number of rows to skip at the beginning of the file

sheet_name.description: Sheet name for XLSX format (required for xlsx, optional otherwise)

fixed_width.description: Whether to use fixed-width column parsing instead of delimiter-based

column_positions.description: Column positions for fixed-width format. Each column must specify position (1-based), width, and optionally type and date_format.

width.description: Width (number of characters) of the field

type.description: Data type of the field (defaults to string if not specified)

date_format.description: Date format string for date fields (e.g., '%Y-%m-%d', '%d/%m/%Y', '%Y%m%d')

json_path.description: Path to records array within JSON structure (for json/jsonl formats). Example: 'export.records' extracts records from {export: {records: [...]}}

record_filter.description: Filter for selecting specific records before parsing (e.g., only records starting with a marker)

marker.description: Regex pattern to match record lines. Only lines matching this pattern are processed.

max_columns.description: Maximum number of field splits for delimited formats. Useful when field values contain the delimiter.

fields.description: Field definitions for the source. Each field defines a column in the parsed data.

calculated_fields.description: Calculated fields derived from other fields using expressions

unpivot.description: Optional unpivoting configuration for this source specifically

then.errorMessage: sheet_name is required when format is 'xlsx'

field.description: Field definition specifying how to parse and type a column

name.description: Field name (becomes column name in parsed data)

type.description: Data type: string (text), integer (whole numbers), decimal (floating point), date (parsed using date_format), boolean (true/false), skip (read but ignore in comparisons)

path.description: Nested path within JSON object for field extraction (json/jsonl formats only). Example: 'localisation.site' extracts record.localisation.site

date_format.description: Date format string using strftime syntax (e.g., '%Y-%m-%d', '%d/%m/%Y'). Required for date type fields.

decimal_precision.description: Number of decimal places for decimal fields (used for normalization and comparison)

calculated_field.description: Calculated field derived from other fields using pandas/NumPy expressions

formula.description: Pandas/NumPy expression to calculate the field. Use field names directly. Example: 'prix_ht * 1.206'

type.description: Data type of the calculated result

rounding.description: Number of decimal places to round result to (for numeric results)

unpivot.description: Configuration for unpivoting (transforming from wide to long format). Converts N pivot columns into N rows with location and value columns.

anchor_fields.description: Fields to keep as anchors (dimensions that remain unchanged during unpivot). These identify the record.

location_field.description: Name of the new column that will contain the original field names (the unpivoted column headers)

value_field.description: Name of the new column that will contain the field values (the data from pivoted columns)

pivot_fields.description: Fields to pivot and their corresponding location labels

location.description: Value to use in location_field for this pivot (e.g., 'PARIS', 'qty_PARIS', etc.)
